title: `podman machine start` cannot find gvproxy
body:
/kind bug

**Error: unable to start host networking: "stat /usr/libexec/podman/gvproxy: no such file or directory"**

I'm trying to install podman on Ubuntu 20.04 following [these instructions](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman:

```sh
  . /etc/os-release
  echo "deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list
  curl -L "https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/Release.key" | sudo apt-key add -
  sudo apt-get update
  sudo apt-get -y upgrade
  sudo apt-get -y install podman
```

2. run `podman machine init`:

    > Error: exec: "qemu-system-x86_64": executable file not found in $PATH

3. install qemu:

    `sudo apt-get -qq -y install qemu-system-x86`

4. try again `podman machine init`:

    > Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-34.20210904.1.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: done

5. run `podman machine start`:

    **Error: unable to start host networking: "stat /usr/libexec/podman/gvproxy: no such file or directory"**

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: unable to start host networking: "stat /usr/libexec/podman/gvproxy: no such file or directory"

**Describe the results you expected:**

No error

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
> I'm trying to install podman on Ubuntu 20.04 

Note that you are following the instructions for macOS.

See https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#ubuntu

If you want to run `podman machine` also on Linux, you can install `gvproxy` from https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock

Eventually it would probably end up being an optional package, similar to the `catatonit` package that provides the `--init` binary
Ah, so I don't need `podman machine` if I'm only using podman as a replacement for docker on Ubuntu? Got it, thanks!
@baude @lsm5 Do we need to start shipping gvproxy in OBS?
imho if ubuntu wants to support machine, gvproxy will have to be added as a dep as well as qemu.
I'm going to close this bug as I dont think there is anything here for the upstream team.  Reopen if you feel otherwise.
